name: Daube De Boeufs Aux Champignons Et a L'orange
recipeIngredient: 2 1/4 kg Stewing beef (cut into large pieces, each about 4 ounces)
6 cl Marc de Provence or Cognac
7 1/2 dl Red wine (sturdy, such as Cotes de Provence)
recipeInstructions:
1. One day before serving the stew, combine the beef with carrots, onions, garlic, parsley, celery, bay leaves, thyme, marc, red wine, and olive oil (1) in a large nonreactive bowl. Tie peppercorns and cloves in cheesecloth; add to the bowl and toss well. Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours, stirring once or twice.
2. Let meat and vegetables return to room temperature. Using a slotted spoon, remove meat from marinade and drain on paper towels. Set vegetables aside.
3. Transfer liquid and cheesecloth bag to a nonreactive large heatproof casserole. Bring to boil over medium-high heat. Boil for 5 minutes to reduce slightly. Remove from heat.
4. Melt butter in olive oil (2) in a large skillet over high heat. When foam subsides, add half of meat and sauté until browned all over, about 5 minutes. Transfer meat to casserole using slotted spoon. Repeat with remaining meat.
5. Sauté reserved vegetables until browned, about 7 minutes. Add vegetables to casserole. Add mushrooms; sauté until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Set aside.
6. Stir tomato paste into casserole. Bring to simmer over medium-low heat. Reduce to very low and simmer, skimming occasionally, until meat is tender, 3 1/2 to 4 hours. Stir in salt and pepper to taste, mushrooms, and orange zest and juice.
7. Discard cheesecloth bag of cloves and peppercorns. The recipe can be prepared up to 3 days ahead and refrigerated. Reheat before serving. Serve with potatoes, rice, or pasta, and the same wine used in cooking (sturdy red wine).
